Title: Natural Variation in TaFAD8-D Promoter Enhances Thermotolerance in Wheat Through Fatty Acid and Lipid Remodelling

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: TAM107; LZ238; Chinese Spring; Fielder; NingChun 4
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: fatty acid desaturase (FAD8), plastid omega-3 desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis; glycerolipid/lipid remodelling
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid chloroplast membrane lipid desaturation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream desaturation step in chloroplast membrane fatty-acid unsaturation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces an omega-3 double bond to convert C18:2 to C18:3 in chloroplast membrane lipids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in leaf and stem; lower in spike and root; downregulated by heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves of 7-day-old seedlings; higher basal expression in CS/NIL-CS than TAM107/NIL-TAM107 under control conditions; downregulated by heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress (mainly 42°C; also promoter assays at 37°C), control 24°C, post-anthesis field heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: C18:3; membrane lipid composition/remodelled chloroplast lipids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane fluidity/integrity regulation, chloroplast and thylakoid stability, photosynthetic efficiency, thermotolerance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf chloroplast/thylakoid-associated membranes; also grain fatty acids assessed
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target fatty acid product and thereby alters downstream lipid species/remodelling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher TaFAD8 activity/expression promotes C18:3 accumulation and lowers the C18:2/C18:3 ratio, which is associated with reduced thermotolerance; loss-of-function or promoter downregulation enhances thermotolerance, photosynthetic efficiency, survival, and thousand-grain weight under heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaFAD8-D was fine-mapped as the candidate gene underlying thermotolerance locus QMpe.cau-2D. 2. A promoter TE insertion in the thermotolerant haplotype lowers TaFAD8-D basal expression. 3. CRISPR loss-of-function of TaFAD8 increased seedling survival, Fv/Fm, and thousand-grain weight under heat stress. 4. TaFAD8 catalyzes the C18:2-to-C18:3 step and modulates lipid remodelling, chloroplast membrane stability, and Ca2+ homeostasis during heat stress.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for C18:2/C18:3 balance under heat stress; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL fine mapping; promoter polymorphism analysis; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase promoter assay; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; fatty acid profiling by GC; lipidomics by LC-MS/MS; transcriptomics; TEM; physiological phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native TaFAD8-D promoters from Chinese Spring and TAM107 in LUC assays; modified TAM107 promoter lacking TE insertion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant penalty in major agronomic traits under normal conditions; beneficial under heat stress with increased thousand-grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000); resequencing/BSA; fatty acid profiling; lipidomics; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter structural variation caused by transposable element insertion suppresses basal transcription of TaFAD8-D
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use the beneficial Hap4 promoter allele with TE insertion or create loss-of-function/editing alleles to improve wheat thermotolerance with minimal yield penalty under normal conditions; suitable for introgression into elite backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: There are three homoeologs (TaFAD8-A/B/D); D-homoeolog promoter variation was mapped genetically, while genome-wide knockout of all homoeologs and D-only editing both improved heat tolerance. Hap4 is rare in modern wheat and likely originated from Aegilops tauschii.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript level gradually increased during heat stress in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves of 7-day-old seedlings; induced by heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via direct binding to W-box motif in TaFAD8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TaFAD8 transcription; knockout increases TaFAD8 expression under control and heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis and lipid remodelling through control of TaFAD8 expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: C18:3/C18:2 balance; membrane lipid remodelling state
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintenance of membrane fluidity/integrity, photosynthetic efficiency and thermotolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TaWRKY71 promotes thermotolerance by repressing TaFAD8; Tawrky71 mutants show higher TaFAD8 expression, lower C18:2/C18:3 ratio, lower Fv/Fm, and lower survival under heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaWRKY71-D was identified from the predicted TaFAD8 regulatory network as a heat-induced TF negatively correlated with TaFAD8 expression. 2. EMSA showed direct binding of TaWRKY71 to the W-box in the TaFAD8 promoter. 3. Dual-LUC assays demonstrated repression of the CS TaFAD8-D promoter by TaWRKY71, while the TAM107 TE-containing promoter abolished this repression. 4. CRISPR knockout of TaWRKY71 increased TaFAD8 expression and reduced heat tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important upstream repressor that fine-tunes the C18:2/C18:3 ratio and thermotolerance through TaFAD8 repression under heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-based candidate identification; RT-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; fatty acid profiling; Fv/Fm and survival assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TaFAD8-D promoters from Chinese Spring, TAM107, and TE-deleted TAM107 in LUC assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes heat susceptibility; no additional tradeoffs reported
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq regulatory network analysis; accession numbers NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintain or enhance TaWRKY71-mediated repression of TaFAD8 for heat tolerance; avoid knockout in breeding for thermotolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The TE insertion in the TAM107 TaFAD8-D promoter abolishes detectable TaWRKY71-mediated transcriptional repression in reporter assays, linking natural cis-variation with altered TF control.
